<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11519632]] Wikidata name: Matsurokoku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Country mentioned in ancient Chinese histories}} {{Use dmy dates|date=December 2023}} {{Citations|date=June 2022}} [[File:Wajinden diagram.svg|thumb|map illustrating the path from the [[:en:Daifang Commandery|Daifeng commandery]] to [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai]], and its distances in the [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]].]]
{{nihongo|'''Matsurokoku'''|末盧國|Matsurokoku / Matsurakoku}} is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first place where [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]], the first place in [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]] where emissaries from [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]] landed on the mainland.
Matsurokoku (末盧國) is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first Japanese land where the envoys of [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] landed on the mainland via [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]]. The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] Saga [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu City]] in the Matsuura region near the sound [[:en:Nabatake Site|Nabatake Site]], in the watersheds of the [[:en:Matsuura River|Matsuura River]], Handa River and Uki River, and in [[:en:Matsurokoku|Matsurokoku]] and [[:en:Ukikunden|Ukikunden Site]] in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]] on the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and [[:en:Geojedo|Geoje Island]] in Korea (about 93 km between [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima]] and [[:en:Busan|Busan]]), and [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]], each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]] in Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Munakata, Fukuoka|Munakata City]], and [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga-gun]].
The size of the area is about that of a [[:en:Commandery (China)|commandery]] or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]], located at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of [[:en:Jōmon people|Jomon]] traits, including [[:en:Dental extraction|extraction]]s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu city]] in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.
== Footnotes == {{notelist}}
== References == <references />
== See also ==
* [[:en:Nakoku|Nakoku]] * [[Itokoku]] * [[:en:Ukikunden|Ukikunden]] {{Yamatai}}

The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11519632]] Wikidata name: Matsurokoku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Country mentioned in ancient Chinese histories}} {{Use dmy dates|date=December 2023}} {{Citations|date=June 2022}} [[File:Wajinden diagram.svg|thumb|map illustrating the path from the [[:en:Daifang Commandery|Daifeng commandery]] to [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai]], and its distances in the [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]].]]
{{nihongo|'''Matsurokoku'''|末盧國|Matsurokoku / Matsurakoku}} is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first place where [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]], the first place in [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]] where emissaries from [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]] landed on the mainland.
Matsurokoku (末盧國) is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first Japanese land where the envoys of [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] landed on the mainland via [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]]. The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] Saga [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu City]] in the Matsuura region near the sound [[:en:Nabatake Site|Nabatake Site]], in the watersheds of the [[:en:Matsuura River|Matsuura River]], Handa River and Uki River, and in [[:en:Matsurokoku|Matsurokoku]] and [[:en:Ukikunden|Ukikunden Site]] in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]] on the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and [[:en:Geojedo|Geoje Island]] in Korea (about 93 km between [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima]] and [[:en:Busan|Busan]]), and [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]], each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]] in Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Munakata, Fukuoka|Munakata City]], and [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga-gun]].
The size of the area is about that of a [[:en:Commandery (China)|commandery]] or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]], located at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of [[:en:Jōmon people|Jomon]] traits, including [[:en:Dental extraction|extraction]]s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu city]] in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. Matsurokoku (末盧國) is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first Japanese land where the envoys of [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] landed on the mainland via [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]]. The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] Saga [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu City]] in the Matsuura region near the sound [[:en:Nabatake Site|Nabatake Site]], in the watersheds of the [[:en:Matsuura River|Matsuura River]], Handa River and Uki River, and in [[:en:Matsurokoku|Matsurokoku]] and [[:en:Ukikunden|Ukikunden Site]] in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]] on the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and [[:en:Geojedo|Geoje Island]] in Korea (about 93 km between [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima]] and [[:en:Busan|Busan]]), and [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]], each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]] in Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Munakata, Fukuoka|Munakata City]], and [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga-gun]].
The size of the area is about that of a [[:en:Commandery (China)|commandery]] or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]], located at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of [[:en:Jōmon people|Jomon]] traits, including [[:en:Dental extraction|extraction]]s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu city]] in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.

Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu city]] in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11519632]] Wikidata name: Matsurokoku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Country mentioned in ancient Chinese histories}} {{Use dmy dates|date=December 2023}} {{Citations|date=June 2022}} [[File:Wajinden diagram.svg|thumb|map illustrating the path from the [[:en:Daifang Commandery|Daifeng commandery]] to [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai]], and its distances in the [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]].]]
{{nihongo|'''Matsurokoku'''|末盧國|Matsurokoku / Matsurakoku}} is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first place where [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]], the first place in [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]] where emissaries from [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]] landed on the mainland.
Matsurokoku (末盧國) is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first Japanese land where the envoys of [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] landed on the mainland via [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]]. The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] Saga [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu City]] in the Matsuura region near the sound [[:en:Nabatake Site|Nabatake Site]], in the watersheds of the [[:en:Matsuura River|Matsuura River]], Handa River and Uki River, and in [[:en:Matsurokoku|Matsurokoku]] and [[:en:Ukikunden|Ukikunden Site]] in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]] on the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and [[:en:Geojedo|Geoje Island]] in Korea (about 93 km between [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima]] and [[:en:Busan|Busan]]), and [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]], each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]] in Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Munakata, Fukuoka|Munakata City]], and [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga-gun]].
The size of the area is about that of a [[:en:Commandery (China)|commandery]] or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]], located at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of [[:en:Jōmon people|Jomon]] traits, including [[:en:Dental extraction|extraction]]s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu city]] in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11519632]] Wikidata name: Matsurokoku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Country mentioned in ancient Chinese histories}} {{Use dmy dates|date=December 2023}} {{Citations|date=June 2022}} [[File:Wajinden diagram.svg|thumb|map illustrating the path from the [[:en:Daifang Commandery|Daifeng commandery]] to [[:en:Yamatai|Yamatai]], and its distances in the [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]].]]
{{nihongo|'''Matsurokoku'''|末盧國|Matsurokoku / Matsurakoku}} is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first place where [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]], the first place in [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]] where emissaries from [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]] landed on the mainland.
Matsurokoku (末盧國) is one of the countries described in ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]'', ''[[:en:Book of Liang|Liangshu]]'' and ''[[:en:History of the Northern Dynasties|History of the Northern Dynasties]]'', and is the first Japanese land where the envoys of [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei]] landed on the mainland via [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]]. The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] Saga [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu City]] in the Matsuura region near the sound [[:en:Nabatake Site|Nabatake Site]], in the watersheds of the [[:en:Matsuura River|Matsuura River]], Handa River and Uki River, and in [[:en:Matsurokoku|Matsurokoku]] and [[:en:Ukikunden|Ukikunden Site]] in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]] on the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and [[:en:Geojedo|Geoje Island]] in Korea (about 93 km between [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima]] and [[:en:Busan|Busan]]), and [[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]], each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of [[:en:Sasebo|Sasebo City]] in Nagasaki Prefecture, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Munakata, Fukuoka|Munakata City]], and [[:en:Onga District, Fukuoka|Onga-gun]].
The size of the area is about that of a [[:en:Commandery (China)|commandery]] or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. [[:en:Yobuko, Saga|Yobuko]], located at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kitamatsuura Peninsula|Kitamatsuura Peninsula]], was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of [[:en:Jōmon people|Jomon]] traits, including [[:en:Dental extraction|extraction]]s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu city]] in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.
== Footnotes == {{notelist}}
== References == <references />
== See also ==

{{nihongo|'''Matsurokoku'''|末盧國|Matsurokoku / Matsurakoku}} is one of the countries described in ''{{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden}}'', ''{{ill|Book of Liang|en|Book of Liang|WD=Q1285767|lt=Liangshu}}'' and ''{{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties}}'', and is the first place where {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei}}, the first place in [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]] where emissaries from [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]] landed on the mainland.
Matsurokoku (末盧國) is one of the countries described in ''{{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden}}'', ''{{ill|Book of Liang|en|Book of Liang|WD=Q1285767|lt=Liangshu}}'' and ''{{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties}}'', and is the first Japanese land where the envoys of {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei}} landed on the mainland via [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]]. The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}} Saga {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu City}} in the Matsuura region near the sound {{ill|Nabatake Site|en|Nabatake Site|WD=Q11619976|lt=Nabatake Site}}, in the watersheds of the {{ill|Matsuura River|en|Matsuura River|WD=Q11531799|lt=Matsuura River}}, Handa River and Uki River, and in {{ill|Matsurokoku|en|Matsurokoku|WD=Q11519632|lt=Matsurokoku}} and {{ill|Ukikunden|en|Ukikunden|WD=Q103944647|lt=Ukikunden Site}} in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From {{ill|Yobuko, Saga|en|Yobuko, Saga|WD=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko}} on the northern tip of the {{ill|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|en|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|WD=Q6417788|lt=Kitamatsuura Peninsula}}, it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and {{ill|Geojedo|en|Geojedo|WD=Q485222|lt=Geoje Island}} in Korea (about 93 km between {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima}} and {{ill|Busan|en|Busan|WD=Q16520|lt=Busan}}), and {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden}}, each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|WD=Q328000|lt=Sasebo City}} in Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata City}}, and {{ill|Onga District, Fukuoka|en|Onga District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156362|lt=Onga-gun}}.
The size of the area is about that of a {{ill|Commandery (China)|en|Commandery (China)|WD=Q517267|lt=commandery}} or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. {{ill|Yobuko, Saga|en|Yobuko, Saga|WD=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko}}, located at the northern tip of the {{ill|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|en|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|WD=Q6417788|lt=Kitamatsuura Peninsula}}, was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of {{ill|Jōmon people|en|Jōmon people|WD=Q11608110|lt=Jomon}} traits, including {{ill|Dental extraction|en|Dental extraction|WD=Q849893|lt=extraction}}s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu city}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.

The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.
== Footnotes == {{notelist}}
== References == <references />
== See also ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]]
{{Short description|Country mentioned in ancient Chinese histories}} {{Use dmy dates|date=December 2023}} {{Citations|date=June 2022}} [[File:Wajinden diagram.svg|thumb|map illustrating the path from the {{ill|Daifang Commandery|en|Daifang Commandery|WD=Q704994|lt=Daifeng commandery}} to {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|WD=Q844295|lt=Yamatai}}, and its distances in the {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden}}.]]
{{nihongo|'''Matsurokoku'''|末盧國|Matsurokoku / Matsurakoku}} is one of the countries described in ''{{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden}}'', ''{{ill|Book of Liang|en|Book of Liang|WD=Q1285767|lt=Liangshu}}'' and ''{{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties}}'', and is the first place where {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei}}, the first place in [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]] where emissaries from [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]] landed on the mainland.
Matsurokoku (末盧國) is one of the countries described in ''{{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden}}'', ''{{ill|Book of Liang|en|Book of Liang|WD=Q1285767|lt=Liangshu}}'' and ''{{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties}}'', and is the first Japanese land where the envoys of {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei}} landed on the mainland via [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]]. The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}} Saga {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu City}} in the Matsuura region near the sound {{ill|Nabatake Site|en|Nabatake Site|WD=Q11619976|lt=Nabatake Site}}, in the watersheds of the {{ill|Matsuura River|en|Matsuura River|WD=Q11531799|lt=Matsuura River}}, Handa River and Uki River, and in {{ill|Matsurokoku|en|Matsurokoku|WD=Q11519632|lt=Matsurokoku}} and {{ill|Ukikunden|en|Ukikunden|WD=Q103944647|lt=Ukikunden Site}} in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From {{ill|Yobuko, Saga|en|Yobuko, Saga|WD=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko}} on the northern tip of the {{ill|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|en|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|WD=Q6417788|lt=Kitamatsuura Peninsula}}, it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and {{ill|Geojedo|en|Geojedo|WD=Q485222|lt=Geoje Island}} in Korea (about 93 km between {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima}} and {{ill|Busan|en|Busan|WD=Q16520|lt=Busan}}), and {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden}}, each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|WD=Q328000|lt=Sasebo City}} in Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata City}}, and {{ill|Onga District, Fukuoka|en|Onga District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156362|lt=Onga-gun}}.
The size of the area is about that of a {{ill|Commandery (China)|en|Commandery (China)|WD=Q517267|lt=commandery}} or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. {{ill|Yobuko, Saga|en|Yobuko, Saga|WD=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko}}, located at the northern tip of the {{ill|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|en|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|WD=Q6417788|lt=Kitamatsuura Peninsula}}, was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of {{ill|Jōmon people|en|Jōmon people|WD=Q11608110|lt=Jomon}} traits, including {{ill|Dental extraction|en|Dental extraction|WD=Q849893|lt=extraction}}s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu city}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.
== Footnotes == {{notelist}}
== References == <references />
== See also ==

There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Matsurokoku]][[ja:末ら国]][[zh:末卢国]]
{{Short description|Country mentioned in ancient Chinese histories}} {{Use dmy dates|date=December 2023}} {{Citations|date=June 2022}} [[File:Wajinden diagram.svg|thumb|map illustrating the path from the {{ill|Daifang Commandery|en|Daifang Commandery|WD=Q704994|lt=Daifeng commandery|1=Daifang Commandery}} to {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|WD=Q844295|lt=Yamatai|1=Yamatai}}, and its distances in the {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden|1=Wajinden}}.]]
{{nihongo|'''Matsurokoku'''|末盧國|Matsurokoku / Matsurakoku}} is one of the countries described in ''{{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden|1=Wajinden}}'', ''{{ill|Book of Liang|en|Book of Liang|WD=Q1285767|lt=Liangshu|1=Book of Liang}}'' and ''{{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties|1=History of the Northern Dynasties}}'', and is the first place where {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei|1=Cao Wei}}, the first place in [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]] where emissaries from [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]] landed on the mainland.
Matsurokoku (末盧國) is one of the countries described in ''{{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden|1=Wajinden}}'', ''{{ill|Book of Liang|en|Book of Liang|WD=Q1285767|lt=Liangshu|1=Book of Liang}}'' and ''{{ill|History of the Northern Dynasties|en|History of the Northern Dynasties|WD=Q1188548|lt=History of the Northern Dynasties|1=History of the Northern Dynasties}}'', and is the first Japanese land where the envoys of {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei|1=Cao Wei}} landed on the mainland via [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]] and [[Iki Island|Iki]]. The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}} Saga {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu City|1=Karatsu, Saga}} in the Matsuura region near the sound {{ill|Nabatake Site|en|Nabatake Site|WD=Q11619976|lt=Nabatake Site|1=Nabatake Site}}, in the watersheds of the {{ill|Matsuura River|en|Matsuura River|WD=Q11531799|lt=Matsuura River|1=Matsuura River}}, Handa River and Uki River, and in {{ill|Matsurokoku|en|Matsurokoku|WD=Q11519632|lt=Matsurokoku|1=Matsurokoku}} and {{ill|Ukikunden|en|Ukikunden|WD=Q103944647|lt=Ukikunden Site|1=Ukikunden}} in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From {{ill|Yobuko, Saga|en|Yobuko, Saga|WD=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko|1=Yobuko, Saga}} on the northern tip of the {{ill|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|en|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|WD=Q6417788|lt=Kitamatsuura Peninsula|1=Kitamatsuura Peninsula}}, it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and {{ill|Geojedo|en|Geojedo|WD=Q485222|lt=Geoje Island|1=Geojedo}} in Korea (about 93 km between {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}} and {{ill|Busan|en|Busan|WD=Q16520|lt=Busan|1=Busan}}), and {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden|1=Wajinden}}, each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|WD=Q328000|lt=Sasebo City|1=Sasebo}} in Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata City|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, and {{ill|Onga District, Fukuoka|en|Onga District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156362|lt=Onga-gun|1=Onga District, Fukuoka}}.
The size of the area is about that of a {{ill|Commandery (China)|en|Commandery (China)|WD=Q517267|lt=commandery|1=Commandery (China)}} or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. {{ill|Yobuko, Saga|en|Yobuko, Saga|WD=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko|1=Yobuko, Saga}}, located at the northern tip of the {{ill|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|en|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|WD=Q6417788|lt=Kitamatsuura Peninsula|1=Kitamatsuura Peninsula}}, was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of {{ill|Jōmon people|en|Jōmon people|WD=Q11608110|lt=Jomon|1=Jōmon people}} traits, including {{ill|Dental extraction|en|Dental extraction|WD=Q849893|lt=extraction|1=Dental extraction}}s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu city|1=Karatsu, Saga}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.

The prevailing theory is that it is a phonetic transcription of [[Matsura-gun]] (anciently "Matsura," {{ill|Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|末羅国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Sueha Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125226}}).
== Outline == Suerokoku is located in the former {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}} Saga {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu City|1=Karatsu, Saga}} in the Matsuura region near the sound {{ill|Nabatake Site|en|Nabatake Site|WD=Q11619976|lt=Nabatake Site|1=Nabatake Site}}, in the watersheds of the {{ill|Matsuura River|en|Matsuura River|WD=Q11531799|lt=Matsuura River|1=Matsuura River}}, Handa River and Uki River, and in {{ill|Matsurokoku|en|Matsurokoku|WD=Q11519632|lt=Matsurokoku|1=Matsurokoku}} and {{ill|Ukikunden|en|Ukikunden|WD=Q103944647|lt=Ukikunden Site|1=Ukikunden}} in the watersheds of the Handa and Uki Rivers, most researchers assume that these sites were included in the central region. From {{ill|Yobuko, Saga|en|Yobuko, Saga|WD=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko|1=Yobuko, Saga}} on the northern tip of the {{ill|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|en|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|WD=Q6417788|lt=Kitamatsuura Peninsula|1=Kitamatsuura Peninsula}}, it is about 28 km to [[Iki Island|Iki]], 73 km between Iki and [[Tsushima Island|Tsushima]], about 75 km between Tsushima and {{ill|Geojedo|en|Geojedo|WD=Q485222|lt=Geoje Island|1=Geojedo}} in Korea (about 93 km between {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}} and {{ill|Busan|en|Busan|WD=Q16520|lt=Busan|1=Busan}}), and {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Wajinden|1=Wajinden}}, each of which is 1,000 ri. Therefore, to be consistent with the distance from Iki, the current names of {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|WD=Q328000|lt=Sasebo City|1=Sasebo}} in Nagasaki Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata City|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, and {{ill|Onga District, Fukuoka|en|Onga District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156362|lt=Onga-gun|1=Onga District, Fukuoka}}.
The size of the area is about that of a {{ill|Commandery (China)|en|Commandery (China)|WD=Q517267|lt=commandery|1=Commandery (China)}} or several counties under the [[Ritsuryō|Ritsuryo system]], indicating that a political power had been formed. {{ill|Yobuko, Saga|en|Yobuko, Saga|WD=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko|1=Yobuko, Saga}}, located at the northern tip of the {{ill|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|en|Kitamatsuura Peninsula|WD=Q6417788|lt=Kitamatsuura Peninsula|1=Kitamatsuura Peninsula}}, was the center of transportation to the continent under the Ritsuryo system, with a stone tomb, a jar coffin, a jar coffin tomb, a box-shaped sarcophagus, and a stone tomb. The site was once controversial for the discovery of {{ill|Jōmon people|en|Jōmon people|WD=Q11608110|lt=Jomon|1=Jōmon people}} traits, including {{ill|Dental extraction|en|Dental extraction|WD=Q849893|lt=extraction|1=Dental extraction}}s and [[:en:Jomon|Jomon]] traits.
Because it corresponds to "Suera prefecture (Matsura no Agata)" and "Matsuura prefecture (Matsuura no Agata)" in " [[Nihon Shoki]] ", it is compared to {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu city|1=Karatsu, Saga}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}<ref>{{Cite web |last=第２版 |first=日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典 |title=末盧国とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%9C%AB%E7%9B%A7%E5%9B%BD-136793 |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> == Description in the Wajinden == In "Wajinden", it is written as follows. There are 4,000 houses, and the mountains and seas are all in the same place. The plants and trees are thick, and you can't see the people in front of you. The water is deep and shallow, and everyone sinks. （There are more than 4,000 houses, and they are located in the mountains and seas, and the plants and trees are so thick that they do not see anyone before them. (Oyi) The people went on through the reed beds so overgrown that they could not see the people ahead of them. The inhabitants caught fish and abalone as seafarers.
== The Ukikunden Site == {{Main|Ukikunden}} The Ukikunden site is a settlement from the Late Jomon and Early to Middle Yayoi Periods, with shell middens, and from the Late Jomon to the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-I earthenware), they built stone tombs on the mountain behind them, and from the Early Yayoi Period (Itatsuki-II earthenware) to the Middle Yayoi Period, they built jar coffins on the flat. 129 pieces have been investigated and 150 pieces have been excavated. A small number of child jar coffins are included in this number. In this jar coffin tomb, a multi-buttoned mirror, 9 fine bronze swords, 5 fine bronze contradistances, 2 fine bronze grenades, copper bracelets, grapeshi, glass tubes, and glass beads were found. Currently, the Kagami area has the largest amount of bronze artifacts imported from the continent found in Japan.{{efn|岡崎敬「多鈕細文鏡－唐津市宇木汲田遺跡」}}。
== Sakura-baba Ruins == This site is located between Hirano-cho and Yamashita-cho in Karatsu City, in the southwest of the dune area north of the Karatsu city center. The jar coffin at this site is from the late Yayoi period, and surveys and excavations of this site were conducted by Okazaki Takashi in 1945 and 1946, and by Sugihara Shosuke in 1955. 1944 (1944) A jar coffin with a jointed mouth was excavated during the construction of an air-raid shelter. Two square rectangular mirrors, a bronze bracelet with hooks, an iron sword, a small glass bead, and a bronze spear were found in the coffin. Human bones were also apparently excavated, but they have been buried back. These artifacts are now in the collection of the Saga Prefectural Museum. In 1955, a jar coffin with a stone lid and three types of earthenware were excavated.
